name: Turkish Straits
climate: Mediterranean transitioning to humid subtropical; mild wet winters and warm dry summers; fog common in the strait

neighbors.name: Black Sea Region
neighbors.relevance: Sole maritime exit for all Black Sea states; grain and energy flows depend on strait transit

neighbors.name: Mediterranean Sea Basin
neighbors.relevance: Western outlet of the straits; connects Black Sea trade to global shipping lanes

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: EU members Romania and Bulgaria depend on strait access for Black Sea exports

neighbors.name: Commonwealth of Independent States
neighbors.relevance: Russia's warm-water naval access and energy exports transit the straits

neighbors.name: Levant
neighbors.relevance: Eastern Mediterranean proximity; Turkey's role in regional energy hub plans

links.description: All Black Sea maritime trade must transit the Turkish Straits under the Montreux Convention
Straits connect Black Sea grain and energy exports to Mediterranean shipping routes
Black Sea cargo transiting the straits continues through Suez Canal to reach Asian markets
Russian oil and gas exports via Black Sea depend on Turkish Strait access
EU-bound Ukrainian and Romanian commodities transit the straits to reach southern European ports
BTC and TANAP pipelines bypass the straits but connect to the same Caspian-to-Europe energy corridor
